# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

**Important Guidelines by the AIPC (All IIT's Placement Committee) to the Recruiters**

In order to have a mutual benefit and to have a long term association, the AIPC has indicated the following guidelines to the recruiters. All the recruiters are requested to kindly stick to these positively to have a smooth placement process.

**Pre-Placement:**

**1. PPOs/PPIs:**This is for students selected as interns through on campus internship hiring process by the recruiters. After successful completion of the internship, it is highly desirable that the status of the PPOs be informed within two weeks of the completion of the internship. Companies are advised to leverage PPO instead of PPI as the latter does not provide significant benefits either to students or companies.

**2. CTC parity:**In order to have a healthy environment, a company is expected to quote uniform CTC for the same job

metadata.title: Aipc-Guidelines

text:
**5. Pre-assessment and final selection tests:**Only one pre-assessment test is allowed for the final placement. Any further assessments (telephonic interview, GD, Personal Interview etc.) should be held on the same day and slot allotted to the company for the final round. Companies are free to decide the mode of pre-assessment tests viz. through virtual / online or through campus visit physically or a hybrid one. In case the mode of assessment is virtual / online, the companies are encouraged to arrange appropriate proctoring from their side. In case of campus visit, it is encouraged that representatives from the company is present in the venue during pre-assessment tests. In case invigilated examination center is required from the institutes, companies need to inform the institute well in advance.

**6. Time duration:** The maximum time for any pre-assessment test (online or off-line) is 90 minutes. Under certain exceptional cases another 30 minutes may be considered. Since the deadl

metadata.title: Aipc-Guidelines

text:
**9. Selection through Hackathon, Case studies:**On-campus internship and full time placement through Hackathon, Case studies competition etc., before the placement phase- 1 season is strictly prohibited. Upon observation of such cases these companies will be debarred from recruitment process in all IITs.

**10. Spot Offers:**Spot offers during the time of personal interview is not permitted.

**Post-Placement:**

**1. Key information on the offers made:** For full time placement offer following may be noted:

- The offer letter must be released within one month from the commencement of result declaration. In case of any deviations or inability to send the offers, suitable information with relevant reason be provided to the concerned Placement/CDC/CCD Office. Under any circumstances the offer letter should not be delayed beyond March.
- The offer letter must contain Date of Joining, Joining location, Detailed breakup of salary structure (including take-home salary) based on the quoted

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

### Dr. Vinit Jakhetiya

#### PIC | Career Development Services

### Message

Dear Recruiters,

We greatly delight in extending you a warm invitation to participate in our institute's Campus Recruitment Programme. With well thought, developed core courses, the curriculum at IIT Jammu emphasizes laying a solid foundation in each discipline. At the same time, the choices of different elective courses going across the disciplines broaden the student's knowledge base. IIT Jammu has transformed into a Center of Excellence in technical education in just over some years to meet the growing demand for technology professionals in India and worldwide.

On behalf of the faculty and the students, I cordially invite you to the recruiting process for our students. I am assured they shall become an asset to your organization.

Happy Recruiting!

Placement Policy - CGPA Cutoff. Policy details: Minimum of 6 CGPA is required for student eligibility in placements.. Category: Eligibility & Registration.
Placement Policy - Attendance Rule. Policy details: Attendance below 80% in guest lectures and workshops will result in being debarred from the first 5 companies.. Category: Attendance Rules.

Placement Policy - Pre-Placement Talk Attendance. Policy details: If a student fails to attend any pre-placement talks/presentations/workshops by a company they applied/pre-registered for, they will not be allowed for that company's process.. Category: Attendance Rules.

Placement Policy - Core vs Non-Core Offers. Policy details: A student can avail one offer in a core job category regardless of CTC. Core placed students can take one more offer in Non-Core (Category III or IV). Non-Core placed students cannot apply to Core.. Category: Application Rules.
Placement Policy - CTC Category I. Policy details: Up to Rs. 8 lakhs per annum CTC. Category: CTC Criteria.
Placement Policy - CTC Category II. Policy details: Rs. 8.01 up to Rs. 15 Lakhs per annum CTC. Category: CTC Criteria.
Placement Policy - CTC Category III. Policy details: Rs. 15.01 up to Rs. 25 Lakhs per annum CTC. Category: CTC Criteria.
Placement Policy - CTC Category IV. Policy details: Rs 25 lakhs and above per annum CTC. Category: CTC Criteria.

Placement Policy - Upgrade Rule. Policy details: Placed students can continue to apply for higher categories if the CTC difference is at least Rs. 3 Lakhs. Upon receiving a second offer in a higher category, further placement processes are blocked.. Category: Application Rules.

Placement Policy - Withdrawal Deadline. Policy details: Students can withdraw their application without limit only before the application deadline. No withdrawals allowed after the deadline.. Category: Rules for Withdrawing.
Placement Policy - Off-Campus Offer Rule. Policy details: Students must inform the CDS Cell immediately if they receive an off-campus offer.. Category: Off-Campus Application Rule.

Placement Policy - Absenteeism Debarment. Policy details: Absenteeism in any test, interview, or selection process results in debarment from the next 2 placement drives.. Category: Absenteeism rules and policy.

Placement Policy - Dress Code (Normal). Policy details: Shirt/T-shirt with collar and trousers (full pants)/jeans for boys; formal attire/trousers/jeans for girls. Flip flops are NOT permitted.. Category: Dress Code.
Placement Policy - AIPC Guidelines. Policy details: All India Placement Committee guidelines followed by IIT Jammu.. Category: AIPC Guidelines.
